prompt: Edit the previous image to make the perceptron concept much more literal and easier for a child to understand, while keeping it physically plausible and cinematic. Keep the same vintage industrial workshop setting and realistic materials, but simplify the mechanism so the cause-and-effect is obvious at a glance. Show several clearly different incoming objects or parcels coming down separate chutes, each with visible weights attached like small metal blocks, all pulling on one central lever or scale arm. Make the central mechanism easy to read: a big balanced seesaw or decision gate that tips decisively to the left or right when the combined pulls become stronger than the opposite side. The output should be visibly binary: two simple gates, one open and one closed, with the lever landing cleanly toward one side. Remove any confusing complexity, extra gears, or abstract machine parts. Make the force flow and threshold effect unmistakable, like a kid could point and say 'those inputs pushed it over and now it chose that side.' No text, no labels, no diagrams, no equations, no UI, no symbolic overlays. Single frame, 4:5 composition, clear subject, dramatic side lighting, tactile metal, dust, bolts, oil stains, realistic mechanical physics, editorial photography style, more literal and obvious than before.
metaphor: a balanced industrial seesaw receiving weighted stones or parcels from several chutes.
